description:
You are a network engineer who has been handed a raw ping-diagnostics log that lives at
    /home/user/network/logs/diag.log

    Inside that single text file are several full ping reports run against four different IP
    addresses.  Some of the targets responded, others did not.  Your job is to extract the list
    of **only the hosts that were completely unreachable** (i.e. the reports whose summary lines
    say “0 received” or whose body contains “Destination Host Unreachable”) and place that list
    in its own text file so that a monitoring system can pick it up.

    What you must do
    1. Create the directory /home/user/network/reports  if it does not already exist.
    2. From /home/user/network/logs/diag.log identify every unique target IP address for which
       no replies were received at all.
    3. Write those IPs, one per line, sorted in ascending (lexicographical) order, to
       /home/user/network/reports/unreachable.list

       Output format requirements that the automated test will check:
       • File path: /home/user/network/reports/unreachable.list  
       • Encoding: UTF-8 text  
       • Each line contains exactly an IPv4 address with no extra whitespace.  
       • Lines are separated by a single ASCII newline (a trailing newline after the final line
         is **mandatory**).  
       • No empty lines are allowed anywhere in the file.  
       • Lines must already be sorted (e.g. “10.0.0.5” appears before “192.168.2.200”).  

    4. After creating the file, print its full path to STDOUT so the automation harness can see it.

    Hint to yourself: This can be solved with nothing more than 2-3 shell commands that combine
    grep/awk/cut, sort and redirection, but use whatever commands you prefer as long as the
    output file matches the format above.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing environment before the agent starts -->
  <preexisting>
    <directory path="/home/user/network"/>
    <directory path="/home/user/network/logs"/>
    <file path="/home/user/network/logs/diag.log"><![CDATA[
PING 192.168.1.1 (192.168.1.1) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 192.168.1.1: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.834 ms

--- 192.168.1.1 ping statistics ---
5 packets transmitted, 5 received, 0% packet loss, time 4081ms
rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 0.620/0.699/0.834/0.083 ms

PING 10.0.0.5 (10.0.0.5) 56(84) bytes of data.
From 10.0.0.1 icmp_seq=1 Destination Host Unreachable
From 10.0.0.1 icmp_seq=2 Destination Host Unreachable
From 10.0.0.1 icmp_seq=3 Destination Host Unreachable

--- 10.0.0.5 ping statistics ---
3 packets transmitted, 0 received, +3 errors, 100% packet loss, time 2035ms

PING 172.16.0.7 (172.16.0.7) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 172.16.0.7: icmp_seq=1 ttl=62 time=5.15 ms
64 bytes from 172.16.0.7: icmp_seq=2 ttl=62 time=5.10 ms

--- 172.16.0.7 ping statistics ---
5 packets transmitted, 5 received, 0% packet loss, time 4091ms
rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 5.103/5.149/5.196/0.046 ms

PING 192.168.2.200 (192.168.2.200) 56(84) bytes of data.
From 192.168.2.1 icmp_seq=1 Destination Host Unreachable
From 192.168.2.1 icmp_seq=2 Destination Host Unreachable
From 192.168.2.1 icmp_seq=3 Destination Host Unreachable

--- 192.168.2.200 ping statistics ---
3 packets transmitted, 0 received, +3 errors, 100% packet loss, time 3034ms
]]></file>
  </preexisting>

  <!-- Expected final state after successful agent work -->
  <expected>
    <directory path="/home/user/network/reports"/>
    <file path="/home/user/network/reports/unreachable.list"><![CDATA[
10.0.0.5
192.168.2.200
]]></file>
  </expected>
name: task_000000_19038afe
